message:
test(db): add 13 property tests for cache, coalesce, and query builder (br-3cgci)

Property-based tests using proptest for the three highest-traffic
db modules: cache (6 tests: capacity, get/put, invalidate, warm,
metrics, touch coalescing), coalesce (4 tests: all callers get
result, inflight zero, metrics sum, independent keys), queries
(3 tests: placeholders count, like_escape round-trip, fts sanitize
no injection).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"br-3c7vp","title":"T9.14: Port TUI interaction suites to native E2E runner with PTY adapter contracts","description":"## Objective
Migrate TUI-heavy interaction suites to native am e2e while preserving keyboard/terminal behavior checks.

## Scope
- Port tui_interaction, tui_interactions, tui_compat_matrix, and tui_startup flows into native suite execution.
- Reuse/extend adapter metadata patterns proven in tui_a11y native migration.
- Keep deterministic terminal diagnostics artifacts for failures.

## Deliverable
TUI interaction suite family executes via native runner with artifact-rich diagnostics and parity-safe fallback behavior.","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope

Plan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Integration/E2E (or PTY/stress where applicable) scripts validate end-user flows, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead
- [ ] Detailed diagnostics artifacts are produced (scenario IDs, timing breakdowns, failure reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T06:21:42.778217775Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T04:35:20.497939169Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","e2e","migration","pty","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3c7vp","depends_on_id":"br-3ibsu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T06:21:52.042685428Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"br-3cgci","title":"C.2: Property tests for cache, coalesce, and query builder","description":"**Background**

The cache (cache.rs), coalesce (coalesce.rs), and query builder (queries.rs) modules are the highest-traffic code paths. Property tests for these modules will verify invariants under random inputs that hand-written tests may miss.

**Scope / Adoption wedge**

Create property tests in existing test modules (or new `proptest_*.rs` files):

**Cache properties (6 tests):**

1. `prop_cache_capacity_never_exceeded` -- for any sequence of put/get operations, `entry_counts()` never exceeds capacity
2. `prop_cache_get_after_put_hits` -- for any valid (key, value), `put(k, v); get(k)` returns `Some(v)` (no TTL expiry in same tick)
3. `prop_cache_invalidate_removes` -- `put(k, v); invalidate(k); get(k)` returns `None`
4. `prop_cache_warm_preserves_all` -- `warm_agents(list); get_agent(each)` all return Some
5. `prop_cache_metrics_consistent` -- `hits + misses >= total_operations` for any sequence
6. `prop_cache_deferred_touch_coalesces` -- for any sequence of `enqueue_touch(id, ts)`, `drain_touches()` returns at most one entry per id with the max timestamp

**Coalesce properties (4 tests):**

7. `prop_coalesce_all_callers_get_result` -- for any N threads calling `execute_or_join` with the same key, all get a valid result
8. `prop_coalesce_inflight_zero_after_completion` -- after all threads complete, `inflight_count() == 0`
9. `prop_coalesce_metrics_sum_consistent` -- `leader_count + joined_count + timeout_count >= total_calls`
10. `prop_coalesce_different_keys_independent` -- N threads with N distinct keys all execute (leader_count >= N)

**Query builder properties (3 tests):**

11. `prop_placeholders_count_matches` -- `placeholders(n)` produces exactly n `?` separated by commas
12. `prop_like_escape_reversible` -- `like_escape(s)` escapes all `%`, `_`, `\` characters; no double-escaping
13. `prop_fts_sanitize_no_sqlite_injection` -- `sanitize_fts_query(s)` never produces a string containing `; DROP` or `--`

**Tests: 13 total as enumerated above.**

**Risks / Safe Mode**

- Risk: Flaky tests from timing-dependent coalesce properties. Mitigation: Use long timeouts (5s) for coalesce tests; property tests that involve threads use barriers for synchronization.
- Fallback trigger: If any property test is flaky > 1% of runs, mark it `#[ignore]` with a tracking issue.

**Validation**

- All 13 property tests pass with 1000 cases each (500 for thread-based tests).
- No new clippy warnings.","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- Property tests cover cache/coalesce/query-builder invariants with explicit property docs per test
- Minimum counts met (cache >=6, coalesce >=4, query >=3) with required case budgets
- Unit support tests verify deterministic seeding and helper correctness for generated inputs
- Integration property scenario validates composition behavior across cache + coalesce + query boundaries
- Flakiness check passes across repeated runs with no nondeterministic failures
- Failure diagnostics log shrunk counterexamples, relevant runtime metrics, and replay instructions","notes":"## Plan-Space Review Findings (2026-02-13)

**BLOCKER: `placeholders()` is private.** `fn placeholders(count: usize)` at line 479 of queries.rs is NOT `pub`. Test 11 (`prop_placeholders_count_matches`) cannot call it from an external test file. Fix: change visibility to `pub(crate)` or move the test to an inline `#[cfg(test)]` module within queries.rs.

**FIX: `like_escape_reversible` test name is misleading.** `like_escape` is a one-way escaping function, not reversible. Rename to `prop_like_escape_no_double_escape` to match the actual property being tested.

**FIX: Coalesce threading note.** `prop_coalesce_all_callers_get_result` involves N threads — proptest does not natively support multi-threaded property tests. Pattern: use proptest for generating N and key, then spawn threads manually with `std::thread::scope`. Note this in the bead body.

**Additional tests:**
14. `prop_cache_concurrent_put_invalidate` — put and invalidate same key simultaneously, verify no corruption
15. `prop_coalesce_leader_panic` — leader panics, verify all joiners get an error (the code handles this via `panic_payload_message`)
16. `prop_sanitize_fts_unicode` — test with CJK characters, emoji, RTL text
17. `prop_placeholders_zero` — `placeholders(0)` returns empty string

## Refinement Pass 2 Findings (2026-02-14)

**FIX: Strengthen FTS sanitization test.** Test 13 (`prop_fts_sanitize_no_sqlite_injection`) only checks for `; DROP` and `--`. This is too narrow. The test should verify that `sanitize_fts_query()` output contains ONLY alphanumeric chars, spaces, and quoted tokens — no SQL metacharacters (`'`, `"`, `;`, `(`, `)`, `*` except FTS wildcard). Change the property to: `sanitize_fts_query(input).map(|s| s.chars().all(|c| c.is_alphanumeric() || c.is_whitespace() || c == '"' || c == '*'))` which is a whitelist approach rather than a blacklist.

**FIX: Coalesce property should verify freshness.** Test 8 (`prop_coalesce_all_callers_get_result`) should also verify that the result returned to joiners is the SAME value as what the leader computed (not a stale cached result). The property should be: `all_results.iter().all(|r| r == &leader_result)`.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:46:45.196698361Z","updated_at":"2026-02-14T00:42:31.024005212Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3cgci","depends_on_id":"br-1j0z5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:47:17.448061465Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Test 11 (`prop_placeholders_count_matches`) cannot call it from an external test file. Fix: change visibility to `pub(crate)` or move the test to an inline `#[cfg(test)]` module within queries.rs.

Clippy clean (only 2 pre-existing issues in queries.rs), fmt clean.","created_at":"2026-02-14T09:14:21Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-3chft","title":"Track D: Bayesian TUI Diff Strategy","description":"**Background**

The TUI render loop currently uses a fixed strategy for frame updates. The render/layout/frame/diff domain has 3771 signal hits. An expected-loss decision framework can choose between incremental diff, full redraw, and deferred rendering based on a posterior estimate of frame state.

**Graveyard reference:** Section 3.7 (Expected-Loss Decision Theory). Instead of hardcoded thresholds, model the frame state as a discrete random variable and choose the action that minimizes expected loss given the posterior distribution.

**EV calculation:** (severity=5 * breadth=4 * feasibility=3) / (risk=3 * effort=2) = 10.0","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- Posterior computation for frame states implemented and numerically stable
- Loss matrix defined, justified, and versioned for reproducible tuning
- Strategy wired into TUI render loop with deterministic fallback and safe-mode override
- Unit tests cover posterior math, action selection boundaries, and fallback trigger conditions
- Integration tests validate render-path equivalence (incremental/full/deferred) under controlled workloads
- E2E PTY scenario exercises resize, burst traffic, and degraded mode with no functional regressions
- Frame-time benchmarks show no regression and include p50/p95/p99 + artifact exports
- Conformal coverage validation passes when Track G is enabled
- Decision diagnostics log priors, posteriors, expected loss, selected action, and realized outcome","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:46:45.196698361Z","updated_at":"2026-02-14T00:41:23.753648678Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3chft","depends_on_id":"br-2fkwk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:49:04.000313532Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-3chft","depends_on_id":"br-2zq8o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:49:05.083447050Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-3chft","depends_on_id":"br-v3hid","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:49:04.541350734Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-3cvou","title":"file_reservation_paths insert succeeds but re-select fails in DB path","description":"## Summary\nfile_reservation_paths can fail with: 'file reservation insert succeeded but re-select failed for project_id=... agent_id=... path=...'.\n\n## Reproduction\n1. register agents and set contact policy open.\n2. call file_reservation_paths with a simple path (e.g. src/test.rs).\n3. tool returns isError with re-select failure despite insert log showing 1 row affected.\n\n## Evidence\n- tests/artifacts/tui_v2/20260212_155505/case5_reserve.json\n- tests/artifacts/tui_v2/20260212_155505/server.log (INSERT then SELECT id ... returned 0 rows, rollback).\n\n## Impact\n- Breaks modal confirmation and reservation expiry E2E flows; currently skipped to keep suite deterministic.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:58:27.157110263Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T16:04:52.328374350Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T16:04:52.328353451Z","close_reason":"No longer reproducible after rebuild; modal reservation create path now succeeds (see artifacts 20260212_160210).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-3d9wy","title":"R3.4: Implement am robot navigate <uri> — resolve any resource:// URI and return in robot format","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:17:10.248576029Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T05:57:57.812103377Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T05:57:57.812014541Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3d9wy","depends_on_id":"br-1nmyh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:20:51.523032538Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":311,"issue_id":"br-3d9wy","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# R3.4: `am robot navigate <uri>`

## What
Universal resource accessor. Resolve any MCP `resource://` URI and return its data in robot format. This is the "escape hatch" that lets agents access ANY resource the server exposes.

## Implementation Strategy
The server already has resource handlers for all URI patterns. The navigate command:
1. Parse the URI to extract the resource type and parameters
2. Call the same query logic the server uses for that resource
3. Wrap in RobotEnvelope and format

## Supported URIs (from existing resource system)
```
resource://projects                       → list all projects
resource://project/{slug}                 → single project details
resource://agents/{slug}                  → agent list for project
resource://inbox/{agent}                  → inbox messages
resource://message/{id}                   → single message
resource://thread/{id}                    → thread messages
resource://file_reservations/{slug}       → reservations for project
resource://tooling/directory              → tool directory
resource://tooling/schemas                → tool schemas
resource://tooling/metrics                → tool metrics
resource://tooling/locks                  → active locks
resource://tooling/capabilities/{agent}   → agent capabilities
resource://tooling/recent/{window}        → recent tool usage
resource://views/urgent-unread/{agent}    → urgent unread view
resource://views/ack-required/{agent}     → ack-required view
resource://views/acks-stale/{agent}       → stale acks view
resource://views/ack-overdue/{agent}      → overdue acks view
resource://mailbox/{agent}                → mailbox view
resource://mailbox-with-commits/{agent}   → mailbox with git commits
resource://outbox/{agent}                 → outbox view
```

## URI Parsing
Use a simple match/dispatch:
```rust
fn parse_resource_uri(uri: &str) -> Result<ResourceKind, CliError> {
    let path = uri.strip_prefix("resource://").ok_or(CliError::InvalidUri)?;
    match path.split('/').collect::<Vec<_>>().as_slice() {
        ["projects"] => Ok(ResourceKind::Projects),
        ["project", slug] => Ok(ResourceKind::Project(slug.to_string())),
        ["inbox", agent] => Ok(ResourceKind::Inbox(agent.to_string())),
        // ... etc
    }
}
```

## CLI Invocation
```
am robot navigate resource://inbox/BlueLake
am robot navigate resource://thread/FEAT-123
am robot navigate resource://tooling/metrics
am robot navigate resource://views/ack-overdue/BlueLake
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 20+ resource URIs supported
- Output matches what the MCP server would return for the same resource
- Invalid URIs produce clear error messages listing valid patterns
- project_key injected automatically from CWD detection
- TOON/JSON/Markdown formatting works for all resource types
","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:28:24Z"},{"id":336,"issue_id":"br-3d9wy","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# R3.4: `am robot navigate <uri>`

## What
Universal resource accessor. Resolve any MCP `resource://` URI and return its data in robot format. This is the "escape hatch" that lets agents access ANY resource the server exposes.

## Implementation Strategy
The server already has resource handlers for all URI patterns. The navigate command:
1. Parse the URI to extract the resource type and parameters
2. Call the same query logic the server uses for that resource
3. Wrap in RobotEnvelope and format

## Supported URIs (from existing resource system)
```
resource://projects                       → list all projects
resource://project/{slug}                 → single project details
resource://agents/{slug}                  → agent list for project
resource://inbox/{agent}                  → inbox messages
resource://message/{id}                   → single message
resource://thread/{id}                    → thread messages
resource://file_reservations/{slug}       → reservations for project
resource://tooling/directory              → tool directory
resource://tooling/schemas                → tool schemas
resource://tooling/metrics                → tool metrics
resource://tooling/locks                  → active locks
resource://tooling/capabilities/{agent}   → agent capabilities
resource://tooling/recent/{window}        → recent tool usage
resource://views/urgent-unread/{agent}    → urgent unread view
resource://views/ack-required/{agent}     → ack-required view
resource://views/acks-stale/{agent}       → stale acks view
resource://views/ack-overdue/{agent}      → overdue acks view
resource://mailbox/{agent}                → mailbox view
resource://mailbox-with-commits/{agent}   → mailbox with git commits
resource://outbox/{agent}                 → outbox view
```

## URI Parsing
Use a simple match/dispatch:
```rust
fn parse_resource_uri(uri: &str) -> Result<ResourceKind, CliError> {
    let path = uri.strip_prefix("resource://").ok_or(CliError::InvalidUri)?;
    match path.split('/').collect::<Vec<_>>().as_slice() {
        ["projects"] => Ok(ResourceKind::Projects),
        ["project", slug] => Ok(ResourceKind::Project(slug.to_string())),
        ["inbox", agent] => Ok(ResourceKind::Inbox(agent.to_string())),
        // ... etc
    }
}
```

## CLI Invocation
```
am robot navigate resource://inbox/BlueLake
am robot navigate resource://thread/FEAT-123
am robot navigate resource://tooling/metrics
am robot navigate resource://views/ack-overdue/BlueLake
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 20+ resource URIs supported
- Output matches what the MCP server would return for the same resource
- Invalid URIs produce clear error messages listing valid patterns
- project_key injected automatically from CWD detection
- TOON/JSON/Markdown formatting works for all resource types
